title: SACRAMENTO, CA – In a major step forward for affordable housing and sustainable growth, the California State Senate today passed SB 358, a bold bill that reduces costly development

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The California State Senate passed SB 358, which reduces costly development fees for housing built near public transit.",
    "SB 358 aims to make it easier and cheaper to build walkable, climate-smart communities.",
    "High traffic impact fees can add up to 20% of a project's total construction cost in areas suited for walking, biking, or transit.",
    "The bill requires at least a 50% reduction in vehicular traffic impact fees for housing near transit.",
    "SB 358 promotes infill development and helps lower the cost of building and renting homes.",
    "The bill aligns traffic mitigation fees with actual traffic impacts.",
    "Housing developments near transit lead to increased walking and transit use while reducing driving.",
    "The 2024 U.S. Supreme Court decision in Sheetz v. County of El Dorado affirmed that development fees must be proportionate to the actual impacts of a project.",
    "SB 358 aligns state law with the precedent set by the Supreme Court while advancing California’s climate and equity goals.",
    "California cities have historically imposed high development impact fees without considering location, resulting in higher housing costs and fewer homes where needed."
  ]
}
```
